title: The Warren Buffett Way
subtitle: The Investment Strategies of the World's Greatest Investor
author: Robert G. Hagstrom
authorBio: Robert G. Hagstrom is a portfolio manager and the founder of Lakefront Capital Management, LLC, a registered investment advisory firm. He is the author of nine books on investing, including The Warren Buffett Way, Latticework, and Investing: The Last Liberal Art. Hagstrom has a B.S. in Economics from Virginia Polytechnic Institute and an M.B.A. from the Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania. He lives in Philadelphia with his family and is a frequent commentator on investment topics.
keyConcepts: Capital allocation as a business owner
notableQuotes: Invest in businesses, not in stocks.
The difference between a good company and a great investment is price.
The key to investing is to determine the competitive advantage of any given company and, above all, the durability of that advantage.
Whether we're talking about socks or stocks, I like buying quality merchandise when it is marked down.
It takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it.
Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.
The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
In the short run, the market is a voting machine but in the long run, it is a weighing machine.
warrenBuffettQuote: Hagstrom has done a superb job of researching and recording the investment principles and practices of the most successful investor of our era.
